blocks.body: Plural nouns are noun forms used for more than one person, thing, idea, or phenomenon. In this topic you will see the main plural patterns (-s, -es, -ies, -ves), irregular forms, nouns used only in the singular or only in the plural, and a short practice set.

{ "title": "11. Short summary", "items": [ "Most nouns form the plural with -s: cat → cats, chair → chairs.", "-es is used after s, ss, x, z, ch, sh: bus → buses, box → boxes.", "Consonant + y → ies, but vowel + y → s: country → countries, toy → toys.", "Many nouns ending in -f / -fe change to -ves: knife → knives, leaf → leaves.", "There are irregular forms that must be memorized: child → children, mouse → mice, person → people.", "Some nouns do not change in the plural: sheep, deer, aircraft.", "Uncountable nouns usually have no plural form: information, advice, weather.", "Some nouns are used only in the plural: trousers, scissors, glasses.", "With plural nouns in general, we often use no article or some, and the when we mean a specific group." ] }
